name: godot_animation_edit
description: Create and modify animations on an AnimationPlayer and preview them in the editor: create, delete, or update animations, add and remove tracks and keyframes, and play, stop, or seek the editor's preview (playback controls the editor, not the running game). Pair each change with an immediate play or seek to check the result; this is the only way to verify animation feel without running the whole game. To inspect animation data without changing it, use godot_animation_read.

action.description:
play: Play an animation
stop: Stop playback
seek: Seek to a position in the current animation
create: Create an animation
delete: Delete an animation
update_props: Update animation properties
add_track: Add a track to an animation
remove_track: Remove a track
add_keyframe: Add a keyframe to a track
remove_keyframe: Remove a keyframe
update_keyframe: Update a keyframe

animation_name.description: Animation name (required for: play, create, delete, update_props, add_track, remove_track, add_keyframe, remove_keyframe, update_keyframe)

custom_blend.description: Custom blend time, -1 for default (for: play)

from_end.description: Play from end for reverse (for: play)

seconds.description: Position to seek to (required for: seek)

update.description: Update node immediately, default true (for: seek)

length.description: Animation length in seconds (for: create, update_props)

loop_mode.description: Loop mode: none, linear, pingpong (for: create, update_props)

step.description: Step value for keyframe snapping (for: create, update_props)

track_path.description: Node path and property, e.g. "Sprite2D:frame" (required for: add_track)

insert_at.description: Track index to insert at, -1 for end (for: add_track)

track_index.description: Track index (required for: remove_track, add_keyframe, remove_keyframe, update_keyframe)

time.description: Keyframe time in seconds (required for: add_keyframe; optional for: update_keyframe)

method_name.description: Method name for method tracks (for: add_keyframe)
